_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 4-5 Pediatric Respiratory Distress (Revised 10/24/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Obstetrics and Pediatrics Protocol Section 4-5: Pediatric Respiratory Distress — MDHHS Approved 10/24/25, Reviewed 2025

sections.title: General Pediatric Respiratory Distress
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 4-5: Pediatric Respiratory Distress — General
points: Follow General Pre-hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
Pediatric patients (< 14 years) utilize MI-MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage — when unavailable, utilize pediatric dosing listed within protocol

Assess the patient's airway: if unable to ventilate after repositioning, refer to Foreign Body Airway Obstruction-Treatment Protocol and/or Airway Management-Procedure Protocol; if respiratory failure or arrest, refer to Pediatric Crashing Patient/Impending Arrest-Treatment Protocol; consider anaphylaxis — refer to Allergic Reaction/Anaphylaxis-Treatment Protocol
Allow the patient a position of comfort that also maintains an open airway
Titrate SpO2 to 94% — have a parent assist with oxygen via blow-by or mask support if needed
Airway should be managed by least invasive method possible
Suction secretions if needed
Consider CPAP if appropriate size available — follow CPAP-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)
Do not delay transport for interventions
Attempt vascular access only if necessary for patient treatment (Specialist skill)

sections.title: Suspected Bronchospasm (Wheezing)
points: Assist the patient in using their own albuterol inhaler if available, prescribed to the patient, and not expired
Administer albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS nebulized (per MCA selection, may be EMT skill) per Medication Administration-Medication Protocol
Consider CPAP if appropriate size available — follow CPAP-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)

In cases of respiratory failure administer epinephrine auto-injector (per MCA selection — MFR): if child appears to weigh less than 10 kg (approximately 20 lbs.), contact Medical Control prior to administering epinephrine if possible; if child weighs 10–30 kg (approximately 20–60 lbs.), administer pediatric epinephrine auto-injector IM; if child weighs > 30 kg (approximately 60 lbs.), administer adult epinephrine auto-injector IM

In cases of respiratory failure administer epinephrine 1 mg/mL IM (per MCA selection, may be BLS or MFR skill): NOTE — BLS not carrying epinephrine auto-injector MUST participate in draw-up epinephrine; if child < 10 kg — contact Medical Control prior to epinephrine if possible; if child 10–30 kg — administer epinephrine (1 mg/1 mL) 0.15 mg (0.15 mL) IM; if child ≥ 30 kg — administer epinephrine (1 mg/1 mL) 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) IM

If available per MCA selection: administer prednisone 50 mg PO to children > 6 years of age; if prednisone not available, patient ≤ 6 years, or patient unable to take PO — administer methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg IV/IO/IM (Specialist skill)

sections.title: Stridor / Suspected Croup
points: Croup is most common in children 6 months to 6 years of age; commonly associated with recent upper airway infection or fever
If foreign body is suspected and unable to be removed: contact Medical Control prior to administration of nebulized racepinephrine/epinephrine — see Foreign Body Airway Obstruction-Treatment Protocol
Consider humidified oxygen

If patient presents with stridor at rest WITHOUT suspected airway obstruction: Paramedic/ALS may administer nebulized racepinephrine/epinephrine per MCA agent selection — Medical Control contact NOT required for stridor at rest without obstruction
Do not delay transport

sections.title: Respiratory Failure or Arrest
points: Ventilate the patient using an appropriately sized BVM with supplemental oxygen — chest rise is the best indicator of successful ventilation
Ventilation rates: Infant: 30 breaths per minute; Child: 20 breaths per minute
Utilize capnography per End Tidal Carbon Dioxide Monitoring-Procedure Protocol to maintain ETCO2 35–45 mmHg (Specialist skill)

BVM is the preferred method of ventilation for patients under 8 years old — when unable to ventilate with BVM and basic airway adjuncts, consider advanced airway per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)
If opioid overdose is suspected: administer naloxone per MI-MEDIC cards; if MI-MEDIC unavailable, administer naloxone per Opioid Overdose Treatment and Prevention-Treatment Protocol
Monitor EKG and refer to Pediatric Crashing Patient/Impending Arrest-Treatment Protocol or appropriate cardiac protocol as required (Specialist skill)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize EMT to administer nebulized albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS for pediatric patients
MCA may authorize MFR to administer epinephrine auto-injector IM for pediatric patients with respiratory failure. MCA responsible for maintaining roster of participating agencies submitted to MDHHS.

MCA may authorize MFR and/or BLS to administer drawn-up epinephrine 1 mg/1 mL IM for pediatric respiratory failure. Personnel must complete MCA-approved training. MCA responsible for maintaining roster submitted to MDHHS.
MCA may include prednisone 50 mg PO as an additional medication option for pediatric patients > 6 years with bronchospasm

MCA must select which Paramedic/ALS agent is used for croup (stridor at rest without obstruction) — this selection does not authorize BLS/EMT administration. Medical Control contact NOT required when the selected Paramedic/ALS agent is used for stridor at rest without suspected obstruction
options: Racepinephrine 2.25% inhalation solution via nebulizer — 0.5 mL in nebulizer diluted with 3 mL NS

key_drugs.name: Albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS Nebulized (Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: 2.5 mg/3 mL NS nebulized — may repeat
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT option available
key_drugs.notes: Assist with patient's own MDI first if available and prescribed.

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine Auto-Injector IM (Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: < 10 kg: contact Medical Control; 10–30 kg: pediatric auto-injector IM; > 30 kg: adult auto-injector IM
key_drugs.indication: Respiratory failure with inability to tolerate nebulizer therapy
key_drugs.contraindications: < 10 kg — contact Medical Control first if possible
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — MFR option available
key_drugs.notes: BLS not carrying auto-injector MUST participate in draw-up epinephrine procedure.

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine 1 mg/mL IM (Draw-Up, Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: < 10 kg: contact Medical Control; 10–30 kg: 0.15 mg (0.15 mL) IM; ≥ 30 kg: 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) IM
key_drugs.indication: Respiratory failure unable to tolerate nebulizer therapy
key_drugs.contraindications: < 10 kg — contact Medical Control prior if possible
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — BLS or MFR option with MCA-approved training
key_drugs.notes: Requires MCA-approved draw-up training. MCA maintains roster submitted to MDHHS.

key_drugs.name: Methylprednisolone IV/IO/IM (Pediatric)
key_drugs.indication: Bronchospasm when prednisone unavailable, patient ≤ 6 years, or unable to take PO
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for weight-based dosing. Parenteral corticosteroid when oral route not feasible.

key_drugs.name: Racepinephrine 2.25% Nebulized (Croup)
key_drugs.dose: 0.5 mL racepinephrine 2.25% inhalation solution in nebulizer, diluted with 3 mL NS
key_drugs.indication: Stridor at rest without suspected airway obstruction (croup) — Medical Control contact NOT required
key_drugs.contraindications: Suspected foreign body — contact Medical Control before use
key_drugs.scope: Paramedic/ALS skill — MCA selects agent; Medical Control contact not required for stridor at rest
key_drugs.notes: MCA must select between racepinephrine 2.25% and epinephrine 5 mg nebulized. This MCA selection is not a BLS/EMT scope expansion.

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine 5 mg Nebulized (Croup)
key_drugs.dose: 5 mg (5 mL of 1 mg/1 mL solution) nebulized
key_drugs.indication: Stridor at rest without suspected airway obstruction (croup) — Medical Control contact NOT required
key_drugs.contraindications: Suspected foreign body — contact Medical Control before use
key_drugs.scope: Paramedic/ALS skill — MCA selects agent; Medical Control contact not required for stridor at rest
key_drugs.notes: MCA must select between racepinephrine 2.25% and epinephrine 5 mg nebulized. This MCA selection is not a BLS/EMT scope expansion.

deterioration_flags: SpO2 < 90% — BVM ventilation; CPAP if appropriate size and respirations > 10; advanced airway if BVM inadequate (Specialist skill)
Respiratory failure with inability to tolerate nebulizer — epinephrine auto-injector or draw-up IM (per MCA selection and weight)
Stridor at rest without obstruction — nebulized racepinephrine or epinephrine by Paramedic/ALS per MCA agent selection; do not delay transport
Suspected foreign body with stridor — DO NOT give nebulized racepinephrine/epinephrine without Medical Control contact; refer to FBAO protocol
scope_notes: Position of comfort, own MDI assistance, blow-by oxygen, SpO2 titration to 94%: all levels
Albuterol nebulized: per MCA selection — EMT option available
CPAP: Specialist skill in base Michigan protocol — EMT scope at MCAs with cpap_bls expansion (if appropriate size available)
Epinephrine auto-injector IM: per MCA selection — MFR option available
Draw-up epinephrine IM: per MCA selection — MFR/BLS with MCA-approved training
Prednisone 50 mg PO (children > 6 yr): per MCA selection
Racepinephrine/epinephrine nebulized (croup): Paramedic/ALS skill; MCA selects agent — no Medical Control required for stridor at rest; not a BLS/EMT scope expansion
BVM ventilation (preferred under 8 years old), advanced airway: Specialist skill
